headline: Two Scattered Spider teenagers admitted crippling London's transit network, while the FortiBleed campaign's Russian operator surfaced wielding 110 million harvested credentials.

emerging_trends.text: Law enforcement is converting years of investigation into convictions and seizures, as two Scattered Spider members admitted crippling London's transit and the Justice Department dismantled the Huione scam empire and an Algerian marketplace operator.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Scattered Spider Members Plead Guilty in London Transit Hack

emerging_trends.text: Defenders are sounding louder alarms over AI, with the Five Eyes alliance warning the danger arrives in months while a fake agent skill slipped past every scanner to reach 26,000 agents.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Five Eyes Warns AI Threat Timeline Is Months

emerging_trends.text: Attackers keep aiming at the software supply chain itself, planting Cordyceps malicious pull requests and PostCSS-impersonating npm packages to seize developer build pipelines.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Cordyceps Pull Requests Poison Developer Pipelines

topics.title: Scattered Spider Members Plead Guilty in London Transit Hack
topics.latest_developments: Thalha Jubair, 20, of London and Owen Flowers, 18, of Walsall pleaded guilty under the Computer Misuse Act on the first day of a planned six-week trial, and a judge will sentence them July 16, 2026.
topics.summary: Scattered Spider operatives breached Transport for London in August 2024, halting service for months and running up £29 million in loss and recovery costs for the agency that runs Greater London's buses, Tube, and trains.

sources.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
Scattered Spider members plead guilty to hacking Transport for London
Two Scattered Spider members plead guilty over cyberattack that crippled London transit
Two Scattered Spider hackers plead guilty over Transport for London cyberattack

topics.title: FortiBleed Campaign Traced to Russian Access Broker
topics.latest_developments: SecurityWeek and Dark Reading identified a Russian initial access broker as the operator, wielding a Golang sniffer that has hit 430,000 FortiGate firewalls and captured 110 million credentials since February 2026.
topics.summary: FortiBleed plants custom sniffers on internet-facing FortiGate firewalls to harvest and validate authentication secrets, exposing thousands of organizations; operators should rotate credentials and harden exposed devices.

sources.title: Russian Initial Access Broker Behind FortiBleed Campaign
FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
What the Fortibleed campaign means for organizations running FortiGate firewalls

topics.title: Five Eyes Warns AI Threat Timeline Is Months
topics.latest_developments: The Five Eyes intelligence alliance issued a joint alert saying AI's cyber risks arrive in months rather than years, the same day security firm AIR revealed a benign fake agent skill that passed every scanner and reached 26,000 agents, and researchers jailbroke Anthropic's safety-tuned Fable 5 within days.
topics.summary: Governments and researchers warn that AI tooling accelerates attacks and slips malicious or jailbroken capabilities past defenses; organizations need visibility into the agent skills and models their staff run.

sources.title: Five Eyes agencies sound alarm about AI's threat to cybersecurity
Fake AI Agent Skill Passed Security Scans and Reportedly Reached 26,000 Agents
Anthropic's Fable 5 Model Jailbroken Within Days

topics.title: Cordyceps Pull Requests Poison Developer Pipelines
topics.latest_developments: Dark Reading detailed Cordyceps, malicious pull requests that abuse CI/CD triggers across Microsoft's Azure Sentinel, Google's AI Agent Development Kit, Apache Doris, Cloudflare's Workers SDK, and the Python Software Foundation's Black, while GitHub hardened actions/checkout against pwn-request attacks and researchers flagged npm packages posing as PostCSS tools to drop a Windows RAT.
topics.summary: Attackers increasingly target the developer pipeline itself through poisoned pull requests, workflow triggers, and trojanized packages; teams should pin actions, restrict the pull_request_target trigger, and vet dependencies.

sources.title: 'Cordyceps': Mushrooming Malicious Pull Requests Threaten Developer Workflows
GitHub Updates actions/checkout to Block Common Pwn Request Attack Patterns
Malicious npm Packages Pose as PostCSS Tools to Deliver Windows RAT

topics.title: Feds Seize Huione and Cybercrime Marketplaces
topics.latest_developments: The Justice Department seized a cloud computing account that subsidiaries of the Southeast Asian Huione Group ran, and separately extradited 26-year-old Abdellah Belmili to face up to 30 years for operating the Market0Day and Spoxy cybercrime marketplaces.
topics.summary: U.S. authorities pressed parallel actions against scam and criminal-market infrastructure, targeting the Huione conglomerate the Treasury cut off from the U.S. financial system last year and an Algerian marketplace operator.

sources.title: Feds seize alleged cyber-scam infrastructure connected to Southeast Asian company
Algerian Man Extradited to US for Running Cybercrime Marketplaces

topics.title: CISA Flags Ubiquiti and Lantronix Flaws Under Attack
topics.latest_developments: CISA added four actively exploited flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog: Lantronix EDS5000 code injection CVE-2025-67038, and three Ubiquiti UniFi OS bugs, CVE-2026-34908, CVE-2026-34909, and CVE-2026-34910.
topics.summary: The additions cover network gear from Lantronix and Ubiquiti widely deployed in enterprises; federal agencies and operators should patch promptly under CISA's binding directive.

sources.title: CISA Adds Four Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog

business_politics.title: U.S.-Iran Deal Snags on Nuclear Inspections
business_politics.latest_developments: Washington and Tehran publicly split Tuesday over whether Iran agreed to U.N. inspections of its nuclear sites, the first crack in the accord the prior briefing reported as President Trump freeing $6 billion in frozen Iranian funds.
business_politics.summary: As negotiators work to permanently end the war with Iran, the United States and Iran disagree over whether Tehran consented to international inspections of its nuclear sites, while a separate plan advanced to break the shipping bottleneck through the Strait of Hormuz and oil futures settled lower on the prospect of returning Iranian crude.

sources.title: Oil Futures Extend Decline in Cautious Trade

business_politics.title: Burnham Set to Demote Reeves as He Builds Cabinet
business_politics.latest_developments: Cabinet ministers lined up behind Andy Burnham Tuesday and reports say he will demote Chancellor Rachel Reeves and review Britain's military investment plan, the cabinet-shaping detail beyond the prior briefing's word that he had a clear path to Downing Street.
business_politics.summary: Andy Burnham, the outgoing Greater Manchester mayor poised to replace Keir Starmer as UK prime minister within weeks, plans to demote Chancellor Rachel Reeves to a lesser role and hand key posts to Manchester allies, and his intent to review the long-awaited defense investment plan sets up a clash with the departing Starmer.

sources.title: Burnham set to demote Reeves to lesser cabinet role
Starmer risks clash with Burnham over defence funding plan
Stream of cabinet ministers throw weight behind Andy Burnham

business.title: Giant Eagle Scrambles After Schwebel's Closure
business.latest_developments: Giant Eagle said it is developing new value-oriented bread options after Schwebel Baking Company's wind-down, since Schwebel's also baked Giant Eagle's store-brand bread—a wrinkle beyond the closure already reported.
business.summary: Schwebel Baking Company's decision to liquidate after more than 120 years pulls both its namesake loaves and Giant Eagle's private-label bread from shelves across western Pennsylvania, and Giant Eagle says it is lining up replacement value bread for shoppers.

sources.title: Giant Eagle reacts to Schwebel's closure, working on more 'value-oriented bread options'

business.title: Fulton Building Reopens as the Atterbury Hotel
business.latest_developments: Downtown's historic Fulton Building reopens Wednesday, June 24, as the Atterbury Hotel after a multimillion-dollar renovation.
business.summary: The 1906 Fulton Building, which architect Grosvenor Atterbury designed for Carnegie Steel magnate Henry Phipps, reopens June 24 as the Atterbury Hotel, part of Marriott Bonvoy's Autograph Collection.

sources.title: The New Atterbury Hotel Opens Its Doors

around_town.title: Chartiers Valley Weighs Outsourcing Buses
around_town.latest_developments: The Chartiers Valley School District board will vote on outsourcing its transportation services, a move the Pennsylvania State Education Association warns could threaten jobs and student safety.
around_town.summary: The Chartiers Valley School District is deciding whether to hand its student transportation to an outside contractor, a step that would affect district drivers and that the Pennsylvania State Education Association opposes on safety grounds.

sources.title: Chartiers Valley school board to vote on outsourcing transportation services

around_town.title: Public Input Sought on Penn State New Kensington
around_town.latest_developments: A community visioning effort opened to decide the future of the Penn State New Kensington campus once it closes.
around_town.summary: Officials launched a public planning process to chart what becomes of the Penn State New Kensington campus in Westmoreland County after it shuts down, inviting residents to shape new uses for the site.

sources.title: What's next for the Penn State New Kensington campus after it closes? You can help decide

around_town.title: ICE Operation at Cranberry Sheetz
around_town.latest_developments: An Immigration and Customs Enforcement operation at the Sheetz station in Cranberry Township on Tuesday morning ended with one person in custody.
around_town.summary: Federal agents conducted an ICE enforcement operation at a Sheetz gas station in Cranberry Township, Butler County, on Tuesday and took one person into custody.

sources.title: ICE operation at Sheetz in Cranberry Township | One person in custody

events.title: Picklesburgh Returns Bigger in July
events.latest_developments: Organizers announced Picklesburgh will run Thursday through Sunday, July 16 to 19, and bill it the largest edition yet.
events.summary: Picklesburgh, the downtown Pittsburgh food festival that USA Today readers have voted America's best specialty food festival four times, returns July 16 to 19, 2026, staged by the Downtown Pittsburgh Partnership across the city's bridges.

sources.title: Special Dill-ivery: Picklesburgh Is Coming

events.title: Andy Warhol Bridge Centennial Bash
events.latest_developments: Organizers released the program for the Andy Warhol Bridge's 100th-birthday party this weekend, adding contests, games, prizes, performances, art activities, and food and drink.
events.summary: The Andy Warhol Bridge, which spans the Allegheny River between downtown Pittsburgh and the North Shore, marks its centennial this weekend with a celebration of contests, games, prizes, performances, art activities, and food and drink.

sources.title: Details for Andy Warhol Bridge Birthday Bash revealed

around_teams.title: McCarthy Wants to Rebuild the 3-4 in Pittsburgh
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette feature laid out how Mike McCarthy wants to model the Steelers' defense on the 3-4 scheme he won a Super Bowl with in Green Bay.
around_teams.summary: The Post-Gazette detailed Mike McCarthy's plan to emulate with the Steelers the 3-4 defense that carried his Green Bay Packers to a title under coordinator Dom Capers, tracing the front's Pittsburgh lineage through Bill Cowher and Dick LeBeau.

sources.title: Mike McCarthy won a Super Bowl with a 3-4 defense. He wants to emulate it with the Steelers.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Cargo Culture
reading.summary: Zitron argues the AI and tech industry has slipped into a cargo cult, imitating the rituals, language, and spending of a successful business while the underlying economics keep failing to deliver.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: Memory Chips and China, Microsoft and Chinese Models
reading.summary: Ben Thompson argues the big three memory makers may come to regret opening the door to Chinese rivals, and that Microsoft has strong incentives to adopt Chinese AI models.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Newport faults AI companies for loudly warning that their own products could be dangerous, comparing it to Ford publishing a whitepaper fretting that its popular F-150 is too perilous to sell.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
